reviews.text:
Trying to build a family through fertility treatment is already exhausting and heartbreaking. What we didn’t expect was to also have to manage a clinic.

Our experience at Create Fertility (St Paul’s, London) has been marked by poor communication and a lack of coordination. The clinic’s “patient coordinator” model hasn’t functioned as a support system for us—there have been long stretches with no proactive contact, no clear ownership of next steps, and uncertainty about who is responsible for our case. Instead of feeling guided, we’ve often felt we were chasing information and stitching the process together ourselves.

Appointments have also felt poorly managed, with delays that add even more strain to days that are already emotionally loaded. When you need answers quickly, getting hold of the clinic can be difficult, and responses are not consistently timely.

If you are pursuing the egg donor route that involves working with IVI Madrid, be aware that coordination between clinics may fall heavily on you as the patient. The contrast has been noticeable: the Madrid-side transfer process has been efficient, and the blood draw staff in London have been consistently good. Unfortunately, the overall London-side support has not matched what this kind of journey demands.

This review is to help future patients know what to expect and to encourage Create to improve the basic standards of communication, continuity, and care.

UPDATE: I emailed a complaint in about the lack of care I'm receiving with hopes that it would help progress our treatment. Instead, I'm told that the the complaint needs to be resolved BEFORE my treatment can resume!! So my treatment hinges on Create fixing poor internal processes that have been on going for years now.  I feel that I am being penalized as a patient for raising a concern.  Despite raising a complaint, I have yet to hear from anyone for days now.  Time is ticking away and we're not closer to having the family we dreamed of.

I would give negative 100 if I could, back in 2024, I was 36 year-old (turining 37) when I under went a full IVF cycle with ABC IVF/Create Fertility. Their one2one conselling session costed me almost £200 per hour and I was not given much information nor support, their service was rushed, and the week when I had my egg retrival surgery, their building was out of power for 3 days. Ended up failed with my non-refundable cycle, they blamed me for being too old and could unlikely never have my own biological child ever. I felt publicly humiliated when the embryologist told me all of these, and so I had formally complained this clinic to HFEA. Then, I had a mental breakdown and suffered 1 month of hospitalisation and further 6 months of recovery. During this time, I had my current boyfriend who encouraged me to try conceive naturally with him. I got pregnant after first try, now I have our lovely daughter in my arms. This clinic is a highly commercialised and they can falsely make you feel good about yourself or have wrong perception about your fertility, so you put your money down to their services. However, their embryologists were incredibly unprofessional and nasty when things aren't going smoothly. BE VERY CAREFUL with choosing your clinic and services if you want to go through full IVFs.

I have been a patient at Create Fertility since September 2024, and I am now in the process of moving my embryos to another clinic. I feel it’s important to share my experience so that others are fully informed of the poor communication and …

An “experienced nurse” did the blood test and used her fingers to wipe my blood instead of the cloth and tape, resulting in the bruise lasting for more than 10 days. Seriously concern around hygiene and professionalism.

If you want to experience the same level of service, feel free to sign on the 3-cycle contract and get you £20k locked in. No refund after 6 weeks of cancellation which blocked me from exploring other clinics in such a time sensitive situation.

CONVEYER BELT SERVICE.
From the moment you start the cycle you feel you are just another part on a conveyer belt in a huge factory. Queues at reception, long waits for blood tests, …

Very poor service with hidden charges that they don't mention on their website such as having to pay to travel to Spain and PAYG medications for donors. I left there wondering if they are scammers so will be contacting the authorities.

It took me a year to recover from the way I was treated in this clinic because of my age and the results.
I had a good promising first consultation.
However,I am aware and informed that the fertility tests good results can be good but does not mean 100% success.i had 10 eggs retrieved and 7 of good grades with 6 of best grades. I Was updated the first 3 days of embryos then afterward received an email that disappeared before I read it on a weekend. Contact the clinic to find out what it was about and was told I will be call the next day or 2days. Anxious to know how my 6 good embryos I was informed of on Thursday and Friday were doing.
When I call in afternoon, I was told that no I am will received the call on Tuesday instead. Call again on Tuesday, I was told I will received a call on Wednesday. On Wednesday I received a call from an ambriologist I never spoken before telling me what do you want us to do with your
one embryo that is left, conduct the biopsy test done on it.
Not even explained to me what happened to the other embryos which were according to the information received on Friday were all growing well with good amount of cells as expected.
When I asked the question, the person.without any consideration of my feelings just said, they dud not survive the biopsy due to your age.
Since then,I did not speak again to either a consultant and the embryologist I have been spoken to before.
The person was so ear close and no emphaty or care  at all when speaking to me about it.
I know I made payment for all ivf procedure from start to the end. I did not have any implantation for which I was asked to pay. They made me me pay for all drugs even progesterone that was supposed to be used when I already pregnant before hands.
I was denied any kind of re-emboursement  for all procedure I did not have.
I paid even for embryos conservation for a year which did not even happened.
I think my care after my embryos got probably accidentally destroyed by someone at that clinic without informing me and missing the dates of my update on the progress was really really unfair.
To have the report of anything else to forward to my GP became another big deal on emails back and forward were I was turned around because they were asking me pay for the report after destroying my embryos.I said that because before the destruction of my embryos I was given every scan and consultant report without even asking for it.
I did find it hard to believed.  That one private care should that worst compared to the public care only because they know that care is not accessible to everyone in the public sector.

Terrible experience.
Where do i start.. in this clinic you are not treated as an individual but a number. There is no personal touches and i felt there was not one consistent thing …

I find it hard to believe that there are reviews for this clinic which are more than 1 or 2 stars. They took weeks of messing us about with requests for various blood tests which we already did and these were sent to them. Communication is …

I was consulted at this clinic five months ago, checked my AMH and scan.In just a few days my results were very different from those from the last clinic. I think this is very abnormal, At my urging, they offered me a free test a month …

I would strongly discourage anyone from engaging with Create Fertility. As a clinic that claims to put patients at the heart of their practice this couldn’t be further from the truth. I faced a number of challenges during my time with the …

Would strongly recommend anyone considering going with Create London to avoid. I can't emphasise enough how poor the communication and service was. I had a positive pregnancy test result but the nurse I spoke to didn't bother to tell me …

Shame Google makes you give one star.

I would like to give a review about Create St Paul’s from a partner’s …
I strongly urge anyone considering CREATE Fertility to think twice before proceeding. Please read my review carefully and reconsider your decision. …

The level of patient care is absolutely appalling.  I’m shocked that a clinic is allowed to treat patients this way.  It’s impossible to get through on the phone and when you do you get through to a receptionist who tells you your patient …

My experience at Create St Paul’s has been a truly stressful one, having undergone IVF previously at other clinics I had experience of the fertility world but nothing prepared me for the terrible service we received at Create, we paid for 3 …

They are extremely unprofessional, unresponsive, and constantly hit you up for more ££. I did not speak to the same consultant twice during 3mo of egg freezing "treatment," they would tell me one thing in the office, then an admin would …

Worst experience with Create! Staff are rude and don’t take your feelings into consideration. My first experience with create was awful. They took advantage of my young age. Promising the world and not taking any of my feelings in to …

I started my IVF journey with Create during Covid and at first, they seemed to be very informative and have time for my questions. But after the egg retrieval was done, I couldn’t get hold of my patient coordinator. …

I had an extremely bad experience with this clinic, at first it was just poor customer service. However, it soon escalated to doctors coming to appointments having not read their notes and needing reminding about the treatment I was having. …

The time of our Zoom consultation was changed by email at 4pm the day before without actually checking if it was convenient for us. We changed our plans to accommodate it and then the Consultant never even came!!!

The website says they help women above 40 to use their own eggs.  The so-called modified IVF plan that they have is for women above 40 with low ovarian reserve.   This is a downright lie, and misleading for women above 40.  I am above 40 …

Avoid this horrible clinic and spend your money in a much better fertility clinic. They treat you like a cash cow and don’t care about their cases. You are not followed by a particular doctor or nurse. They are all lost and confused. Then …

All of good reviews are fake, they only care of taking your money not your mental health and emotions, they always apply low doses to get few number of eggs unless I had to many follicles but they want you to repeat the process two or three …

Awful, never again, if you use the clinic in Manchester good luck, the consultant turned up late to both consultations, no one even bothered to contact us of delays, and once things were moving forward we were promised to timelines that …

Very frustrating as we were treated as customers not patients a lot of hidden fee and a lot of medication sent before starting the treatment while there wasn’t even need to use as they told me after initial scans the medication is not …

Absolutely shocking they have zero care and compassion!! Definitely wouldn't recommend! We use Manchester create fertility and couldn't fault them they make you feel so comfy in there clinic but st Paul's London is a whole different story. …

I had a very poor experience after visiting CREATE (St Paul's). The Dr that assessed me had clearly not reviewed my notes and seemed to be questioning what I was telling her. Her manners were rude like she would interrupt you (not a big deal) .She then said she had to scan me to assess my situation. After a long while she said my case was complex, she could not see properly my organs blaming my severe endometriosis. She mentioned that in order for them to tell me how they could help I would need a further scan and other tests that she did not explain. I mentioned I would need to discuss with my husband as she believed I would have to choose egg donation. She then instructed me to go to the reception and book my scan and to take give the receptionists my notes. What was my surprise that despite me having already paid for the initial consultation plus the scan, they were expecting me to pay for another scan just because that Dr "couldn't see due to the severity of my condition". They also wanted me to pay for other tests that I clearly said I would need to first talk to my husband. Of course, I did not pay, I excused myself and repeated to staff I would first need to talk to my husband. Had I paid what they wanted to that initial consultation would have cost me more than 1000 pounds! and they would just see if they could help. Some days later they got in touch with me saying she had discussed with other Drs how they could help and they wanted me to book another consultation to discuss. (Why they were looking for more money in advance then?). I decided not to continue any dealings with them. I decided to go for a clinic abroad who were able to help me at the firs appointment and scan I had with them. A real world of difference and treatment. This other clinic made me feel like a human and not an ATM. I would recommend people to stay away.

Avoid avoid avoid! Since going to another clinic it's made me realise just how bad Create were. Felt very much like just another number to them. Different doctors all the time, always running late. phone lines constantly busy. Very little support and understanding given. Miss-leading prices. IVF and infertility is hard enough, do yourself a favour and pick a different clinic!

If you can find a different clinic, please choose that instead of CREATE. I have had the worst experience with the clinic at St.Paul's. Mentally really exhausted and cannot think of doing any private treatment because of it. Every time I think of the experience, it gives me heart palpitations. It was all about money and still waiting to receive the call about the refund.

Had some blood tests here recently. The actual test was not a pleasant experience. They have a corridor queuing system which was a bit like a factory. It wasn't explained to me so a couple of people got ahead of me. It felt so impersonal and took a lot longer than I expected as I was originally given an appointment time. Worst of all I was told the results would probably be with me the next day. It's now 6 days later. I have chased for the results now 5 times now by phone and email. I've not received anything other than 'we've passed your enquiry on' on the phone and no reply at all by email. There's been no acknowledgement of the bad service, not even one 'oh I'm sorry about that'. I paid £365 for these tests and have nothing to show for it yet.

Absoultely appalling service.

Two rounds of ivf, absoultely no communication between us and the staff at all. Patient coordinator was useless. We are still waiting on a refund from over 6 months ago.

We have never felt so helpless about having a child as we have done with them and they do not have a care in the world just want more money out of us

The worst hospital, the doctor will never be found, I spent 30k to do IVF at CREATE Fertility, during the period of doing IVF, I was overwhelmed, scared, doctors and nurses are not professional, they never care whether you can succeed or not,  I don't even consider your feelings. If you want to waste money and fail to do IVF, CREATE Fertility is the best choice. I am so disappointed. The five-star reviews are all fake. I hope no one will be fooled again.

On a round of FET, a cyst was discovered from a bad cocktail of drugs (so much for low dosage), it was obvious it was because of the drugs but the course proceeded for another week where the cyst got bigger with more drugs. The cycle was eventually abandoned and within hours, was sent a bill for “abandoned cycle fee”. Bedside manner non existent.  Empathy non existent. Patient coordinators are debt collectors only

Absolutely appalling. Different Dr’s every time. Changing doses every time. The administration of my care is worse than non existent. When they try they get it completely wrong. I’m still waiting since April for an outcome for a decision on a refund as they messed up my protocol, the only message I’ve had responded to something irrelevant to the query at hand. How can they be trusted to offer good quality care?

The poor costumer service and communication between the different  departments is shocking and made us feel like another number in the big money business that the IVF world is. From day 1 when I went for my 1st Ultrasound they didn't even …

I was seen at the ST Paul’s site . Unfortunately I didn’t have a good experience at this clinic. My consultation was 10-15 minutes and I paid £150 . In my opinion there seems  to be no pastoral care. I note that the media team at Create try …

We went to Create in St Pauls for a couples fertility screening and would not reccommend this clinic to anyone. In fairness, the doctor who carried out the consultation explained fertility in clear, user-friendly terms. However, I found the …

Private clinic delivering NHS level service. Having received way better service and treatment at my new clinic all I can say is stay away from this clinic. Find somewhere better. Waste of time & money. …

Extremely rubbish service.
They are really useless and time wasters and wasted 2 hours before the useless doctor seen us. …

I had a very bad experience with Create, I cancelled my 3 cycle treatment due to to mismanagement, carelessness from the consultant. They wanted to make money without giving true insights about your situation. After cancellation I haven't received my refund yet. Its been months, number of emails but no update. Very disappointed.

I experienced a very bad thing in this hospital. The hospital management was very chaotic. During the process of IVF treatment, no one answered the phone calls to the hospital, and I couldn't find any doctor in charge of you. It was very frustrating and frustrating. 100% not recommended, please don't waste money on this hospital again.

One word for their service: MESS. What a mess!!!! The staff is so confused and confusing that everything seems out of its place.
The way they make money is due to desperation of couple in getting a child, and they charge you separately for every sing thing, gigantic amount for a cycle that you believe it won't cost much at first instance, but then once you are on the loop, the invoices just keep coming, coming and coming. The reception service is terrible (Raynes Park), the clinic isn't the most hygienic place and if you don't push them, you don't get things done. Unless of course, they want to charge you, then they will make anything to get your money. DONT FALL IN THE TRAP.

I was so excited about this clinic, but after 3 rounds I realised they don’t care about patients. They provide no direction, they have no point of view... All they care about is getting money from there patients and not providing guidance. I am going in for my last FET with such a bad feeling unfortunately. Please save your money and go somewhere else.

All they after is money they dont care about their patiants you will not see same doctor evary appoinment is defferent doctor.
But what is the worst in my case i had polpy in the uterus they sow it my first …
A very rubbish clinic with irresponsible management. They will only press you for money and will not deal with the problem. If you want to throw money away, you can go to them.

I have upload my experience which they have removed it and they requested more information regarding i have all recipes and call history which they don’t respond when they said pls ring me regarding your experience and when i call to talk receptionist put me hold for 15 minutes after disconnected my call very bad service don’t trust

I went for three mild IVF cycle.Keep changing doctor every time I visited the doctor. Wrong diagnosed and gave wrong protocols with the negative result at the end. Very disappointed and heartbroken. Please avoid.
Unsympathetic staff read out all the pricing to me on my first meeting, each add on price made treatment more expensive than anywhere else, I left there in tears.
Am so upset and disappointed in this clinic  they just want money they don’t care about you or you getting pregnant please don’t go there
If there is any negative ratings they are worth it.They are full of fraudsters beware of create fertility
Very poor and unprofessional communication from the clinic doctor
Terrible service. Don't wast your money here.
Very disappointing. I would not recommend it.

⭐️ 1 Star

I would strongly urge anyone considering CREATE Fertility to think very carefully before proceeding.

Our experience has been characterised by poor communication, a complete lack of coordination between departments, and a level of patient care that has steadily deteriorated over time.

We were led to believe we would be proceeding with a frozen embryo transfer this cycle, took time off work for appointments, and only at the last minute were told that basic prerequisite steps had not even been arranged. These were things that should have been identified and coordinated well in advance. The result was an entirely avoidable delay to treatment.

What has been most shocking is the complete inability to get hold of our assigned patient coordinator. Despite repeated emails, phone calls, formal complaints, and requests for escalation, weeks have passed with virtually no meaningful communication. Multiple staff members acknowledged internally that responses were overdue and that management had been “chased”, yet still nobody contacted us.

The repeated explanation given to us has been that the clinic is “exceptionally busy”. Frankly, if that is the case then the clinic has clearly taken on more patients than it can adequately support and manage appropriately. As private patients undergoing fertility treatment, we do not believe it is unreasonable to expect basic communication, coordination, and continuity of care.

Every department appears to operate in isolation, with responsibility constantly shifting elsewhere while no one takes ownership. The role of a patient coordinator should surely be to bridge those gaps, yet in our experience that coordination has been entirely absent.

Sadly, this sits within wider concerns we have had regarding clinical decision-making and informed consent. We were advised to freeze one embryo at day 3, while the remaining embryos continued in culture but ultimately did not progress to blastocyst stage. This frozen embryo is now our one and only embryo.

Looking back, we do not feel we were provided with sufficiently balanced or patient-specific information to make a truly informed decision regarding freezing at day 3 versus continuing culture to day 5. We were later advised there is no clear evidence base demonstrating improved outcomes with this approach, yet the implications of the decision — emotionally, financially, and practically — were never properly explained to us.

Most concerningly, during a follow-up appointment after egg collection, the consultant openly acknowledged that the clinic receives “lots of complaints” regarding this practice and stated they “might be changing” it. Hearing that after the fact only reinforced our feeling that concerns around this approach were already well known internally, yet we were not properly informed beforehand.

As non-medical patients, you place enormous trust in a fertility clinic to guide you through hugely emotional and complex decisions. Instead, we have been left dealing with months of uncertainty surrounding the viability of our only embryo, while continuing to pay storage fees and repeatedly chase for basic communication.

At times, the clinic has felt less like a healthcare provider focused on patient care and more like a profit-driven operation, where communication and support rapidly decline once patients are within the system. The repeated delays, lack of accountability, ongoing storage fees, and constant need for us to chase updates have only reinforced that feeling.

At this stage, we feel completely powerless. CREATE currently has our one and only embryo in storage, while we struggle simply to get responses or move treatment forward. We are now actively exploring transferring our embryo to another clinic because we no longer have confidence in the level of care, communication, or organisation being provided.

Fertility treatment is emotionally exhausting enough without feeling ignored, unsupported, and passed between departments. We would not recommend this clinic.

Create Fertility have sent me a full patient records of someone else (including IDs, scans, tests, transfer info etc...) while I am still waiting for a number of missing records for myself. I have emailed them back, but no-one is responding …

I’ve had the worst experience ever at this clinic - I’ve wasted a year of my life here. I’d encourage you to go elsewhere. Firstly if you’re hoping to use the clinic in Hertfordshire they are shut for most of the week so you’ll mainly be in London. Then all your appointments will get messed up and your patient coordinator won’t email you back. The administrative abilities of this company are abysmal. No personalised appointments, seeing different nurses and doctors the whole time. I’d give a 0 if I could. Save yourself the effort and go elsewhere Create are a joke. They didn’t even check in after my latest transfer - which was also unsuccessful!

We purchased three IVF cycles based on the advertised price, but ended up paying nearly double due to constant extra charges—especially for scans.

Communication and care were very poor. On the day of egg collection, we were suddenly asked about freezing embryos, something no one had properly discussed with us before. We received no real guidance, and then were told it was “too late.”

We felt rushed and dismissed throughout, as if they just wanted to take our money and move on. The professionalism and empathy—especially from the owner—were extremely disappointing.

After more than 10 IVF cycles elsewhere, this has been our worst experience by far. What seems cheap at first becomes much more expensive with hidden extras. Avoid this clinic.

If you have a lot of money, you can give it to this hospital. Before treatment, everything seems fine, but after you pay, you can’t find anyone responsible. It’s a terrible IVF clinic. I would absolutely not recommend it.
I chose Create Fertility St Paul’s because I am over 40 and the clinic promotes strong advertising regarding support .However, my overall experience was disappointing. …

Terrible communication thoughout our entire IVF journey. Still chasing SAR request after 33 days of consent submission, told every time I call that someone will call back but never does.  This was our first IVF process and feel totally let …
I will write a more detailed review shortly about my experience with three rounds of egg freezing. For now, I want to share my disappointment with the annual storage payment process. …

For a clinic that supposedly specialises in the over 40s, it is beyond poor that they do not do more to encourage uptake of PGTA. This is one of our regrets after two wasted years of transfers via this clinic. Initially if you have relatively few embryos it is tempting to bypass it, but this is a false saving as it can cost you in so many other ways. They are the ones who should know that, but never had any advice from them on that point. Doctors and facilities fine though patient coordination variable. Prices (relatively speaking) reasonable. But you are on a conveyor belt and get standard treatment, no attention to male vitamins and you could well end up wondering whether there are investigations they haven't tried.

I am sad to say that although when I originally reviewed Create in 2021 I rated it very highly I have had to come back and edit my review.
I received treatment in 2021 and my experience with all staff right from …

I sadly have to give CREATE Cheapside a low rating.
We have just finished our second unsuccessful round of IVF with the team here, and I will not be using them if we chose to have another go. …

The reason why I am leaving two starts (rather than one) is because the doctors and nurses were actually very good. Apart from that our experience with create was very bad. We were seen by different people each time and it felt like no one …

We choose this clinic because they have a branch not too far from where we live (an hour drive) as well as based on their published success rates and positive reviews. It was our first experience doing a round of IVF and to begin with we …

Took a long time to get over the bad treatment I received by the staff (non doctors) at this clinic. Rude, unkind and unprofessional. They treat you like you're a burden to them although the cost is not that cheap. Avoid this clinic!!
Firstly, I would like to point out that we had a successful cycle of IVM and my wife is now pregnant. …

Admin and support were painful at every step of our unsuccessful journey. When requesting refunds around unsuccesful cycles they took MONTHS (in fact I am still awaiting refund a month after notifying them that I was ended the process, and …

My friend has attended the treatment at St Paul.
We were not that impressed as the consultants seems do not be  receptive  or well informed about new researches in IVF , and my feeling is that they are doing …

I'd like to express that I feel disappointed finding out about the procedure Create takes when deciding/offering to freeze an embryo on day 3. When we received the call to ask as if we would like to freeze that embryo, we were told that it was a good quality. Later one, we found out that this embryo would still have to go to a previous and additional process of thawing before being transfer in which there is a possibility it could be lost, i.e. it cannot have a good quality to be transfer. During one of the last calls, I had with a nurse, I was told that this is normal procedure of Create. To decide to freeze embryos on day 3. I was also told that other clinics do not do this anymore, while Create still does. I felt disappointed when given this information as this can be easily interpreted as being given higher hopes. This procedure, I was told, is done to prevent or to make sure there is one embryo frozen. However, this is not 100% accurate as at that moment, we as a couple, or other couples, do not know that there is an additional step required for the embryo to be transferred. In other words, I feel like I have two embryos frozen (chances for a potential pregnancy) and one maybe. As you can imagine, this feels disheartening considering the high financial investment and effort put into this procedure.  Furthermore, the day 3 embryo, after three days of cultivation, it did not survive and was discarded. The quality was not good enough to freeze again. We keep asking ourselves why wasn't it cultivated from the beginning further? We have ask Create and we still get no answer. We've been told that this was in perfect quality to freeze and it was done just in case none of the other would survive. This creates uncertainty. I interpret is as a way to say add an extra 'successful' frozen embryo as a result of the treatment when in reality it isn't! Therefore, I highly suggest to couples to let their embryos develop until day 5 or until they are in great quality for direct transfer.

Regarding the communication, I agree with other comments here. The communication in English of some foreign professionals is poor. I am a foreigner as well, working in a foreign country; however, I make the effort or have had courses to help me achieve a better performance. The fixed line has had technical issues, but the main problem was their language skills. If these people are communicating such sensitive information to couples, some actions could be taken by Create (i.e. training) to improve.

Regarding the service in St Paul's, all has been well. Just some slight delays in early morning appointments 5-10 min late.
The team does stay in touch, calls/emails to follow-up on the procedure, and the staff is very friendly/amicable.

My chose of stars is related to the disappointment of the guidance for freezing embryos. It would be better to be more transparent and open for this matter with the couples.

Update 14.12.21:
After this review, we received a call from the lead consultant. We must say that our comments were always taking into consideration and we did receive some additional feedback before. However, in some cases, the extra info provided, did not make things better. After this new and last call  on Nov. 22, my husband and I felt satisfied with the explanations that were given.

Nevertheless, my comments and rating above remain. Reviews are meant to help the company improve and to share experiences with future customers. I've have stated the good and the bad, and I hope that this helps couples to inform themselves well about this part of the process and also Create to improve their customer service.

Communication is key, and this was part of the problem, technically (with the lines) and the way is was delivered (communications skills/fluency in the language)

We had failed IVF. We had 2 transfers and both came negative. The reason is that the staff does not even have the common sense to do semen analysis before the treatment. They did blood tests of my wife but no semen analysis for me. Later I came to know that they want to charge extra £1000 for ICSI by saying the sperm motility is not good. Those who are going to this clinic, please make sure you have done all the tests otherwise you will end up losing the money or unsuccessful IVF. I still dontunderstand how can a fertility clinic start treatment without doing semen analysis.

As someone who is enrolled in this clinic and still awaiting the results of our treatment I feel that now is the right time to offer my balanced and fair feedback of this place.  They have also encouraged us to leave feedback in order to …

After one round of fertility with Create I have already decided they are not the clinic I wish to continue with.  I agree with a previous comment there is no personal touch to the service you are just a commodity and it
is not tailored to the individuals needs there is not enough transparency with costs too. Example is over stimulation and excess of medication sent out when you may not be able to use and you cannot send back. Please do  your research its important and not just on recomendation.

Long unnecessary waiting times. Many of the workers can't speak clearly enunciated English. You can sense the disorganisation.

Invoicing seems a focus of the service rather than ensuring a smooth efficient process that respects the time of clients.

Underground laboratorial setting has no natural light and feels overly “clinical”.

Location is very good and ideally placed in central London..

Out of principle, it's worth saying that this business model is exploitative and takes full advantage of desperate people looking to conceive. It's a money extraction racket backed by government statutes that will set people back thousands for an outcome that isn't even guaranteed.

By them making home DIY artificial insemination less legally protected, the gov is funnelling people into this disgusting cash cow industry and taking their cut through taxes.

Also IVF has a much higher rate of a child emerging with significant issues. This is because IVF selects imperfect “lazy” sperm to fertilize an egg. Nature, as usual, does things better.

In response to their comment on this review; I am on the system, but I've chosen to post this review anonymously.

I received treatment with Create St Paul’s in June of 2025 and since moving clinics wanted to leave a review of my experience. I’ll start by saying that all of the healthcare professionals I encountered there were very kind, considerate and extremely supportive in helping me through my first experience of IVF.

After a lot of research we decided to go with Create. A huge deciding factor in this was its location with it having a clinic in St Albans as well as central London. I have a fairly hectic work schedule so this was important to minimise stress around apts. Unfortunately throughout the process we only had two apt. in St Albans one of which was cancelled so I would not take this location as much to do with the clinic.

The difficulty we had with create was around the clinic being very unorganised and lacking in personalised care. You see a different Dr at every appointment leading to a lack of consistency. The biggest issue for us was the patient coordinators. The idea is you are assigned someone who supports you throughout the process who helps with all of the admin side.

Our patient coordinator changed 4 times and were completely unorganised to the point where I often had to follow up and chase up admin and apts. This added a lot more stress into the process, managing everything alongside work and I felt I couldn’t trust them to be on top of everything.

Following our unsuccessful results the doctors did a review and said this was a case of ‘luck’ and they recommended we go again with the same protocol. Knowing what I know now, I am absolutely baffled with this response as there were a whole host of easy diagnostic tests we could have done and things we could have tweaked moving forward. We have since found a different clinic where we had drastically different positive results.

Looking back at the experience with create, I feel they are a very run of the mill IVF clinic that won’t be able to go above a very basic IVF protocol. They offer treatment for NHS and private patients however, I feel we very much got an NHS experience but at the price of a private clinic.

Hello Luca,

We're sorry to hear you've ben unhappy with your experience with us. Our team aim to provide quality communication and care to all patients and we apologise if you felt this was not the case for you. We're like to reassure you that members of our team are investigating the circumstances of your review and will reach out to you regarding this.


Wishing you all the best in the next steps of your journey,

CREATE Fertility
